plainEnglishSummary.whatWeMeasured: We look at each player’s first few events each season and see if they improve or decline over those early starts.
plainEnglishSummary.slopeMeaning: For SG Total, a positive slope means the player tends to gain more strokes as the season progresses (improving).
plainEnglishSummary.returnToFormMeaning: Return-to-form index estimates the event number where the player is expected to reach their prior-5-season baseline performance.
plainEnglishSummary.howToUse: If the return-to-form index is high, the player likely starts slow and improves later; if it is low or zero, they’re ready early.

exampleNarratives: Putnam, Andrew tends to reach baseline by event 0.0 (based on 6 seasons), indicating early-season readiness.
Gooch, Talor tends to reach baseline by event 0.0 (based on 4 seasons), indicating early-season readiness.
Seiffert, Chase tends to reach baseline by event 0.0 (based on 3 seasons), indicating early-season readiness.
Phillips, Chandler typically reaches baseline after event 6 (avg 317.3 over 2 seasons), suggesting a later peak.
Simpson, Webb typically reaches baseline after event 6 (avg 283.4 over 6 seasons), suggesting a later peak.
Dumont de Chassart, Adrien typically reaches baseline after event 6 (avg 98.0 over 2 seasons), suggesting a later peak.
